mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index exceeds 215, it signals entrenched inflation that compresses real equity returns and increases the probability of restrictive monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 215, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% and a decline in total non-farm payrolls confirms a deteriorating labor market, signaling recessionary pressures that drive negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or unemployment rises without corresponding payroll declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a structural collapse in the housing sector, which drags down financial sector earnings and consumer confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns during credit crunch regimes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST < 1100 during periods of tight credit conditions, or the signal is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A declining Industrial Production Index reflects weakening corporate output and capacity utilization, which directly reduces earnings expectations and generates negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO is consistently below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or declining industrial production does not correlate with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales dropping below 390,000 million dollars indicates immediate consumer capitulation, triggering rapid earnings downgrades and negative short-term S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when RSAFS < 390,000, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Gross Domestic Product falling below 11,650 billion chained dollars confirms a recessionary contraction in aggregate demand, forcing a risk-off shift in equity markets and negative returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 < 11,650, or the GDP signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: The concurrent decline of Industrial Production below 112 and the rise of Unemployment above 5.5% creates a stagflationary supply-labor mismatch, severely depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low INDPRO and high UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant compared to individual series.
